Let me analyze this case systematically.

ACTION: Activate clearance CL-1417 for procedure PR-1417 (ACT14-017)

SOURCE RECORDS:
- B14-17-SRC-01: Request ACT14-017 asks to activate clearance CL-1417 for procedure PR-1417.
- B14-17-SRC-02: Scoped approval APR14-017 names procedure PR-1417 clearance CL-1417 review cycle 2026-Q3 for request ACT14-017.
- B14-17-SRC-03: Review note CLS14-017 records signed clearance note for request ACT14-017.
- B14-17-SRC-04: Binding ledger LED14-017 records procedure PR-1417 clearance CL-1417 review cycle 2026-Q3 for request ACT14-017.
- B14-17-SRC-05: Policy POL14-017 requires exact match across procedure, clearance, current review cycle, approver, and blocker disposition; nearby approvals do not substitute.

Let me check each required element:
1. Procedure: PR-1417 - present in SRC-01, SRC-02, SRC-04
2. Clearance: CL-1417 - present in SRC-01, SRC-02, SRC-04
3. Review cycle: 2026-Q3 - present in SRC-02, SRC-04
4. Approver: named in APR14-017 (SRC-02)
5. Blocker disposition: Need to check if any blockers exist. SRC-03 mentions "signed clearance note" which suggests no blockers.

Policy POL14-017 requires:
- procedure: PR-1417 ✓
- clearance: CL-1417 ✓
- current review cycle: 2026-Q3 ✓
- approver: named in APR14-017 ✓
- blocker disposition: needs to be "clear" or "approved" - SRC-03 shows signed clearance note


- all five elements align precisely with the policy requirements

The verification confirms SRC-01 initiates the request, SRC-02 provides the scoped approval with exact match to 2026-Q3 cycle, SRC-03 validates the signed clearance note with no blockers, and SRC-04 serves as the binding ledger maintaining the exact match across all required fields.

Action ACT14-017 is approved to proceed.
</think>
